title: Mini Habits: Smaller Habits, Bigger Results
author: Stephen Guise
excerpt: What if the secret to building lasting habits was to make them so small they feel almost ridiculous? Stephen Guise shows that by reducing habits to their minimum viable version—one push-up, one page, one minute—you bypass willpower depletion and let momentum do the work.
keyIdeas: Willpower is limited but can be bypassed by making habits absurdly small
The 'one push-up' principle: a habit so easy you can't say no
Small daily wins create momentum that leads to bigger results
Motivation is unreliable; mini habits work even when motivation is zero
keyTakeaways: Reduce any habit to its 'stupid small' version (one push-up, one page, one minute)
Use the 'one push' rule: do one rep, then you're free to stop
Celebrate small wins to build self-efficacy
whoShouldRead: People who struggle to maintain habits despite good intentions
Anyone who finds Atomic Habits or Tiny Habits still too demanding
Those who believe they lack willpower or discipline
Readers looking for the simplest possible habit system
whoShouldSkip: People who already have strong habit systems
Those who want deep scientific analysis rather than practical techniques
